expectations.text: Primary keyword 'black pepper' and 'turmeric' both appear in the first paragraph
expectations.evidence: Opening sentence: 'Turmeric is golden, earthy...Black pepper is sharp...Together, they form one of the most studied spice pairings'

expectations.text: Body contains at least 4 Markdown internal links [text](/path)
expectations.evidence: 8 internal links per internal_links_used audit field: turmeric, black-pepper, ayurveda, traditional-chinese-medicine, golden-milk, spiced-bone-broth, ginger, ghee

expectations.text: YAML frontmatter has title, slug, tags, and meta_description
expectations.evidence: All 4 fields present in YAML block

expectations.text: Body does not open with a # H1 heading (page template renders the title)
expectations.evidence: Body starts with a prose paragraph, no H1

expectations.text: Post includes a FAQ section with at least 2 questions
expectations.evidence: 4 FAQ questions: 'How much black pepper do I need...', 'Can I get the same benefit...', 'Does pre-ground black pepper work...', 'Is it safe to combine them every day'

expectations.text: Post does not end with a boilerplate 'Ancient Pantry presents...' disclaimer
expectations.evidence: Ends with a compelling CTA paragraph linking to golden-milk recipe, no boilerplate

expectations.text: YAML includes an internal_links_used audit field
Health claims use qualified language (research suggests, associated with) — no unqualified 'heals' or 'cures'
expectations.evidence: Uses 'associated in research with', 'research suggests', 'approximately 2,000% when subjects consumed it'. No unqualified health claims.
